name: Les McCann
abstract: Leslie Coleman McCann (September 23, 1935 – December 29, 2023) was an American jazz pianist and vocalist. He is known for his innovations in soul jazz and his 1969 recording of the protest song "Compared to What".

concerts.label: Jazz Concert: Billy Paul, Les McCann, and Randy Crawford

Nancy Wilson, Singer, and Les McCann, Piano

about_quotes.speaker: JJ Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee (JJ Johnson) does not mention Les McCann directly; the relationship is between Johnson's son Kevin and Les McCann.
about_quotes.doc_title: JJ Johnson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John Levy
about_quotes.evidence: I was managing Les McCann and I was manager of Roberta Flack at that time. ... I was close with Les McCann. He and I were together most of the time over then.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Levy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Joel Dorn
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with Mose © Fillius Jazz Archive -5- Allison, you know I worked with Herbie Mann a little bit and then Les McCann and then Eddie Harris.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joel Dorn interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: seeing people like Cannonball Adderley, Eddie Harris, Les McCann, Rahsaan Roland Kirk. Getting all of that early is what inspired me to do it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 25, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ... Les McCann ... what was that? ... [INTERVIEWEE] Oh yes. But then they had . . . all those people had very lean left hands.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: Eventually I met Les and mentioned to him that Souplet told me that he and I would play a set together the last night of the festival.
